title: MeshRemoveFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing mesh remove features in a component and supports the ability to create new mesh remove features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a mesh remove feature. Works only in parametric mode.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a MeshRemoveFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the mesh remove feature you want to create and then use the add method, passing in the MeshRemoveFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified mesh remove feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified mesh remove feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of mesh remove features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a mesh remove feature. Works only in parametric mode.
add.return_description: When successfull, a MeshRemoveFeature is created for each MeshBody that was input. An array of the created MeshRemoveFeature objects is returned.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A MeshRemoveFeatureInput object that defines the desired mesh remove feature. Use the createInput method to create a new MeshRemoveFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the MeshRemoveFeatureInput object) to define the removal.

classType.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a MeshRemoveFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the mesh remove feature you want to create and then use the add method, passing in the MeshRemoveFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created MeshRemoveFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: inputBodies
parameters.description: A array with mesh bodies in a parametric design.

item.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified mesh remove feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified mesh remove feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of mesh remove features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshRemoveFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshRemoveFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: MeshRemoveFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshRemoveFeatures_var.objectType
